message:
feat(rchd): add operator convergence commands: status, dry-run, repair (bd-vvmd.3.5)

Wire RepoConvergenceService into DaemonContext and expose three new API
endpoints for operator observability and repair:

- GET /repo-convergence/status — full convergence dashboard with per-worker
  drift state, budgets, remediation hints, and aggregate summary counts
- GET /repo-convergence/dry-run?worker=<id> — simulate convergence without
  side effects, reporting whether sync would be attempted
- POST /repo-convergence/repair?worker=<id> — reset worker convergence state,
  bypassing hysteresis, clearing synced repos and restoring budgets

Adds repair_worker() to RepoConvergenceService for hysteresis-free operator
repair. Includes 15 API handler tests and fixes all clippy warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -423,10 +423,10 @@
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","title":"Implement daemon RepoConvergence service (convex-hull repo set + drift detection)","description":"Create service that computes required repo hull for active builds, compares worker state, and drives convergence actions through the adapter. Must expose drift state and convergence outcomes for scheduling decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- RepoConvergence service computes and tracks required repo convex-hull state per scheduling context.
- Service maintains explicit drift states with deterministic transitions and hysteresis rules.
- Convergence decisions are bounded by time/attempt budgets to preserve fail-open behavior.
- Service emits structured state-change events for observability and operator tooling.
- Integration tests validate healthy convergence, partial drift, auth failures, and stale-state recovery.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.663944951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:35:05.704331106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:35:05.704256056Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["convergence","daemon","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.599381364Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.397218582Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.484662133Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":149,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RepoConvergence service should expose deterministic states (ready, drifting, converging, failed, stale) with timestamps and reason codes. Keep convergence idempotent and bounded per worker to avoid starvation. Include explicit separation between policy decisions and execution so dry-run/status surfaces can use the same core logic.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:24Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: wired additional repo_updater convergence command surface into runtime path in rch hook pre-sync flow. Added command-aware idempotency keys and execution sequencing: SyncDryRun (read-only preflight) -> SyncApply (mutating convergence) -> StatusNoFetch (post-apply snapshot), all with strict fail-open behavior on validation/transport/adapter errors. Added helper coverage tests: test_repo_updater_command_name_is_stable and test_build_repo_sync_idempotency_key_for_command_distinguishes_commands. Validation performed: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace --all-targets; tests/e2e/path_dependency_fixtures.sh; tests/e2e/repo_updater_contract.sh.","created_at":"2026-02-17T01:59:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","title":"Integrate pre-build repo presence/freshness checks into worker selection flow","description":"Before assigning a worker, ensure required repos exist and meet freshness criteria. If not, trigger bounded sync or select alternate worker; otherwise fallback local per policy. Acceptance: missing-repo failures are prevented proactively.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Worker selection preflight verifies required repos are present and sufficiently fresh before remote build start.
- Preflight consumes convergence state and evidence rather than implicit assumptions.
- Missing/stale repo outcomes trigger explicit local fallback with reason-coded diagnostics.
- Decision logs include repo-level evidence and final selection/fallback rationale.
- Tests validate selection behavior under healthy, drifting, and unknown convergence states.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:04.866291287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T05:29:21.416554233Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T05:29:21.416487789Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["preflight","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","selection"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.254572590Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.994130539Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:14.797213178Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.455098267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","title":"Add background periodic convergence and drift alerting","description":"Implement background reconcile loop that keeps worker repo hull warm and reports sustained drift. Must avoid interfering with active builds and respect rate limits/time budgets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background convergence loop runs at controlled cadence with workload-aware throttling.
- Loop avoids interfering with active builds and respects configured safety budgets.
- Sustained drift emits actionable alerts with ranked remediation suggestions.
- Alert noise controls (debounce/hysteresis) prevent flapping false alarms.
- Integration tests cover steady-state, prolonged drift, and recovery-to-healthy transitions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.067406758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:07.776122601Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alerts","maintenance","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.194944166Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","title":"Add operator commands: convergence status, dry-run, and repair","description":"Expose clear CLI/API surfaces for repo convergence visibility and manual remediation. Include machine-readable JSON mode and human-readable remediation guidance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Operator surface provides convergence status, dry-run simulation, and targeted repair actions.
- JSON output is stable and machine-parseable; human output includes concise remediation guidance.
- Repair operations are auditable, scope-constrained, and safe against untrusted paths.
- CLI paths preserve stdout/stderr mode guarantees and hook-safe behavior.
- Integration tests validate command behavior across healthy, drifting, and failed convergence states.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.269501534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:45:08.048857613Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","operations","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.394101401Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","title":"Add operator commands: convergence status, dry-run, and repair","description":"Expose clear CLI/API surfaces for repo convergence visibility and manual remediation. Include machine-readable JSON mode and human-readable remediation guidance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Operator surface provides convergence status, dry-run simulation, and targeted repair actions.
- JSON output is stable and machine-parseable; human output includes concise remediation guidance.
- Repair operations are auditable, scope-constrained, and safe against untrusted paths.
- CLI paths preserve stdout/stderr mode guarantees and hook-safe behavior.
- Integration tests validate command behavior across healthy, drifting, and failed convergence states.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.269501534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:09:06.729877705Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","operations","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:15.394101401Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","title":"Add integration/E2E tests for repo convergence orchestration","description":"Validate repo convergence behavior end-to-end with deterministic fixtures and the shared reliability harness. Cover adapter parsing, drift detection, bounded convergence execution, and fail-open behavior under network/auth/staleness/partial-update faults.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenarios cover nominal convergence, stale-repo repair, partial-update interruption, and adapter failure classes.
- Worker-selection decisions after convergence are asserted against expected readiness outcomes.
- Fail-open behavior is validated when convergence exceeds time budget or policy constraints.
- Logs capture convergence state transitions, selected remediation, and scheduling impact.
- Tests run via shared E2E harness with reproducible seeds and artifact retention.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.471705864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.899923941Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.2.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.656032500Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:19.206699348Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:19.410110788Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:26.849899079Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.899849291Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:13.525367699Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","title":"Add repo trust/allowlist policy for convergence scope","description":"Define and enforce an explicit allowlist policy for repos eligible for automatic convergence/sync. Prevent accidental sync of unrelated/sensitive paths and ensure convergence requests are constrained to approved roots under /data/projects. Include operator override workflow with audit trail.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Allowlist policy defaults to deny and restricts convergence to approved roots under `/data/projects`.
- Policy explicitly covers `/dp` alias handling and rejects traversal/out-of-scope targets.
- Override workflow requires explicit operator intent with durable audit trail metadata.
- Policy violations produce deterministic reason codes and non-ambiguous remediation guidance.
- Unit/integration tests validate allow, deny, override, and malformed-policy scenarios.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:25.040681503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T00:39:11.513444614Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T00:39:11.513425297Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["policy","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:28.241160720Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:28.041951568Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":137,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: repo convergence without an explicit trust boundary can unintentionally sync repos outside intended scope. This task establishes a security and governance guardrail for automated convergence.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","title":"Add exhaustive unit/integration tests for RepoConvergence internals","description":"Add deep unit and integration coverage for repo convergence internals: adapter response parsing, drift-state transitions, bounded retry behavior, policy allowlist enforcement, and worker-selection handoff semantics. Include deterministic mocks for repo_updater failure modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover adapter success/failure taxonomy (timeout, malformed JSON, partial result, auth/network failure).
- State-machine tests cover `ready/drifting/converging/failed/stale` transitions and hysteresis rules.
- Integration tests verify `bd-vvmd.3.2` + `bd-vvmd.3.3` behavior under missing/stale repo conditions.
- Tests assert fail-open local fallback semantics when convergence cannot be proven within budget.
- Structured logs for each test include scenario id, expected decision, actual decision, and remediation code.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:32:22.268118209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T05:30:52.979704192Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.533185718Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.742878308Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.958451949Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:31.168562531Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.692259600Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added to close a quality gap: repo_updater integration previously had strong E2E coverage but no explicit deep unit/state-machine test bead. This prevents regressions in convergence logic and adapter parsing.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:48Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","title":"Add exhaustive unit/integration tests for RepoConvergence internals","description":"Add deep unit and integration coverage for repo convergence internals: adapter response parsing, drift-state transitions, bounded retry behavior, policy allowlist enforcement, and worker-selection handoff semantics. Include deterministic mocks for repo_updater failure modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover adapter success/failure taxonomy (timeout, malformed JSON, partial result, auth/network failure).
- State-machine tests cover `ready/drifting/converging/failed/stale` transitions and hysteresis rules.
- Integration tests verify `bd-vvmd.3.2` + `bd-vvmd.3.3` behavior under missing/stale repo conditions.
- Tests assert fail-open local fallback semantics when convergence cannot be proven within budget.
- Structured logs for each test include scenario id, expected decision, actual decision, and remediation code.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:32:22.268118209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:08:29.115974733Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:08:29.115864607Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.533185718Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.742878308Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:30.958451949Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:33:31.168562531Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.692259600Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":155,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added to close a quality gap: repo_updater integration previously had strong E2E coverage but no explicit deep unit/state-machine test bead. This prevents regressions in convergence logic and adapter parsing.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:34:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","title":"Define secure credential flow for repo convergence operations","description":"Define secure credential and identity flow for repo convergence actions on workers (keys/tokens/host validation), including rotation, least-privilege scope, and failure handling. Prevent convergence failures caused by auth drift while keeping security posture strict.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Integration contract specifies auth mechanism(s), secret source, rotation process, and permission boundaries.
- Worker-side credential validation and host identity checks are explicit and testable.
- Failure taxonomy distinguishes auth/authorization/host-verification errors with remediation guidance.
- Convergence operations fail closed for security violations but preserve RCH fail-open for compilation execution fallback.
- Unit/integration tests cover expired/revoked/invalid credentials and host-mismatch scenarios with structured logs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:39:45.401554181Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T00:50:00.750469744Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T00:50:00.750451109Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["policy","rch-rel-2026q1","repo_updater","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.071872224Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.3.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:13.282336508Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":162,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.3.9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"User-outcome rationale: repo convergence failures are often auth/identity problems in practice; adding explicit credential lifecycle design prevents silent drift and flaky remote readiness.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4","title":"Workstream: Disk Pressure Resilience via storage_ballast_helper","description":"Purpose: prevent build failures from full disks by integrating `/data/projects/storage_ballast_helper` policy logic into worker maintenance and scheduling decisions. Add proactive pressure detection, safe reclaim actions aware of active builds, and admission control that avoids selecting workers likely to fail from low space.

## Success Criteria
- Pressure monitor + policy engine classify worker disk risk with stable, low-noise signals.
- Reclaim actions are conservative, auditable, and safe for active builds/high-value caches.
- Scheduler admission control consistently avoids high-risk workers while preserving fail-open behavior.
- Disk-pressure recovery tests demonstrate prevention and recovery across unit/integration/E2E paths.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:00.828438642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T09:04:57.918788814Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T09:04:57.918769097Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["disk-pressure","rch","rch-rel-2026q1","storage","workers"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:22:25.853550290Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:24:22.305201835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","depends_on_id":"bd-vvmd.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:40:15.502437331Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":133,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Safety principle: storage remediation must be pressure-aware and build-aware. Reclaim operations that can invalidate active builds or high-value caches are disallowed unless policy-approved. Selection/admission control are first-class prevention mechanisms.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:20:21Z"},{"id":144,"issue_id":"bd-vvmd.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Storage reliability policy: treat disk pressure as a first-class scheduling signal, not just cleanup after failures. Reclaim order must preserve active builds and high-value caches, and actions must be conservative/auditable. Admission control should steer builds away from risky workers before no-space errors happen. Recovery must never rely on destructive broad deletion, and must preserve fail-open semantics for user workflows.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:28:06Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-vvmd.4.1","title":"Decide storage_ballast_helper integration mode with ADR","description":"Evaluate direct crate integration vs CLI adapter for storage_ballast_helper. Document tradeoffs (latency, coupling, observability, upgrade risk), choose approach, and define safety constraints for reclaim operations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ADR compares crate-integration and CLI-adapter modes across latency, coupling, safety, and upgrade risk.
- Decision includes explicit failure containment and rollback strategy.
- Chosen mode defines clear interface contract and ownership boundaries.
- ADR documents observability hooks required for disk-pressure decisions.
- Approval criteria include sign-off from reliability and operations perspectives.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:19:05.680083453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T03:03:46.291914376Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T03:03:46.291895661Z","close_reason":"ADR completed: docs/adr/006-storage-ballast-helper-integration.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adr","design","rch-rel-2026q1","storage_ballast_helper"]}
